evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows OursModel winning on all 5 benchmarks with error bars; legend and axis labels are readable at single-column width.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are set; font.family='sans-serif' avoids DejaVu default in principle, but pdffonts confirms the embedded font is 'DejaVuSans' (the default sans-serif on this system), so the DejaVu Sans default was not overridden to a preferred publication font.
evidence.palette: Okabe-Ito subset used (#999999, #56B4E9, #E69F00, #D55E00); no red/green directional misuse; hatch patterns added for B&W accessibility.
evidence.layout_density: y-axis starts ~26 (not 0) per data range; 3.5x2.6-inch single-column figure; bars fill the panel well; legend fits in upper-left without overlapping data.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; legend is inside the axes at upper-left; error bars explained in caption; no obvious issues a Reviewer 2 would flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real PDF (version 1.4, 1 page) with embedded subset TrueType font (CID TrueType, emb=yes, sub=yes); text is selectable; fonttype=42 is set.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (39 KB), figure.png (71 KB), figure.svg (177 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all four methods and five benchmarks, states OursModel achieves highest accuracy on all five benchmarks, and explains error bars (std over 5 seeds) and palette rationale.

overall_strengths: Clean Okabe-Ito palette with hatch patterns ensures colorblind and B&W accessibility, satisfying both color and pattern differentiation requirements.
Figure is correctly sized for single-column publication (3.5 in wide), fonttype=42 is set for editable PDF text, and all required output files (pdf, png, svg, caption, script) are present.
overall_weaknesses: Despite font.family='sans-serif' being declared, the embedded font resolves to DejaVu Sans (the matplotlib default on this system) rather than a preferred publication font such as Helvetica or Arial; a minor quality issue.
The legend is placed inside the axes (upper-left), which partially overlaps the Baseline and Method-A bars for MMLU—marginally acceptable but could be moved outside or to a lower-conflict location.
